chapters.name: Spec & Design
chapters.summary: SEP_INVOICING_SPEC_v1.md — 21 clients, rate cards, weight/piece-challan/NOS-to-weight billing, Sales Register + GSTR1 CSV, five tabs.
chapters.content:
## Why a Separate App

SEP Invoicing exists because invoicing does not belong inside a workforce management tool. The Dashboard tracks attendance, production, and costs — operational data that changes daily. Invoicing is a financial workflow: client master data, rate cards, billing calculations, GST compliance, and government-mandated export formats. The data models, the users, the regulatory requirements — everything is different.

The decision to build a standalone PWA rather than add tabs to the Dashboard was made on 1 April 2026, the same day the restructuring took effect. With clean policies defining the billing process for the first time, the spec could be written against documented procedures rather than observed habits.

## The 8-Pass Spec

SEP_INVOICING_SPEC_v1.md was the first commercial application to go through the full SPEC_ITERATION_PROCESS. Previous applications of the 8-pass framework had been for baby tracking features (SproutLab) and project management (Codex). Applying it to invoicing exposed a different class of risks: regulatory compliance (GST rules on rounding, invoice numbering, GSTIN validation), financial accuracy (rate cards with effective dates, three distinct billing modes), and data integrity (invoice numbers must never collide, even after localStorage reset).

## 21 Clients, Three Billing Modes

The client master encoded 21 active and inactive clients with full GSTINs, HSN/SAC codes, and rate cards. Three billing modes emerged from the real-world complexity of zinc electroplating job-work:

Weight-based billing — the default. Material arrives, gets weighed, gets plated, gets weighed again. Invoice charges per kilogram at the client's rate.

Piece-challan billing — SSS Mehta's model. Material arrives as counted pieces on a challan. Each challan becomes a line item. Rate is per kilogram, but the weight is calculated from piece count multiplied by standard piece weight.

NOS-to-weight billing — Samarth Engineering's model. Material arrives as numbered items (NOS) with known standard weights per piece. The system converts NOS to kilograms using the standard weight table, then bills at the per-kg rate.

Each mode required different form fields, different calculation logic, and different validation rules. The spec documented all three completely, including edge cases (what happens when standard weight is missing, when challan count is zero, when rate effective date is in the future).

## Pass 4-6: The Bug Hunt

Pass 4 catalogued 10 bugs in the existing v2 invoice generator — inline onclick handlers, wrong FY suffix logic, inconsistent SAC codes, malformed GSTINs, missing design tokens. Pass 5 identified 8 drift risks: invoice number collision on data loss, rate revision mid-month, FY rollover, localStorage size limits. Pass 6 resolved 13 builder questions, including the critical decision that gstRound() (proper rounding) would be used instead of Math.floor — GST rules require standard rounding, not the conservative floor used in SproutLab's baby-tracking context.

## The Five Tabs

The spec defined five tabs: Home (dashboard), Create Invoice (the primary workflow), Incoming Material (receiving log), Invoice Register (search and filter), and Client Master (reference data). GST export was placed inside Invoice Register as an action, not a separate tab — it is a derivative of invoice data, not a primary workflow.

chapters.name: Phase 1 Build
chapters.summary: Client Master, Create Invoice, Settings. 21 bugs found and fixed across three QA rounds.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Phase 1 delivered the three core tabs that make invoicing functional: Client Master, Create Invoice, and Settings. The goal was simple — by the end of this phase, Rishabh should be able to create a real invoice for a real client with correct GST calculations.

Client Master embedded all 21 clients with their GSTINs, rate cards, billing modes, and active/inactive status. The data was hardcoded in the initial seed (not fetched from an API) because the client list changes rarely and the app needed to work offline on a factory floor.

Create Invoice implemented the full billing workflow: select client, add line items (with mode-specific fields), calculate subtotals with GST (18% split as 9% CGST + 9% SGST for intra-state, or 18% IGST for Spectooling's Maharashtra shipments), generate invoice number with FY-aware series, and save to localStorage.

Settings provided the configuration layer: business details (GSTIN, address, bank account for invoice printing), invoice series management, and data backup/restore.

## The Architecture

The split-file architecture was adopted from day one — no monolith-to-split migration tax. 22 modules in build.sh, each with clear responsibility boundaries. The inv- CSS class prefix kept all styles greppable and collision-free. data-action delegation from the first line of JavaScript.

The design system adapted the SproutLab-proven token architecture but with SEP's industrial personality. Different domain, same engineering discipline.

## 21 Bugs, Three Rounds

The post-build QA audit found 21 bugs across three rounds. Round 1 caught the structural issues: a form submission that bypassed validation, a GST calculation that used toFixed() instead of gstRound(), and a client selector that did not filter inactive clients. Round 2 found integration issues: invoice numbering that did not persist across browser sessions, a rate card lookup that failed on the NOS-to-weight billing path, and dark mode tokens that were defined but not applied to the new components. Round 3 found cosmetic issues and declared the build stable.

The three-round pattern confirmed what SproutLab had already proven: the first round catches what you expected to break, the second catches what breaks because of the first round's fixes, and the third confirms the dust has settled.

chapters.name: Phase 2 Build
chapters.summary: Invoice Register and GST Export tabs.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Phase 2 added the two remaining data tabs: Invoice Register and GST Export. With Phase 1 handling invoice creation and Phase 2 handling invoice retrieval and compliance reporting, the core invoicing workflow was complete.

## Invoice Register

The register implemented searchable, filterable access to all saved invoices. Filter by client, by date range, by payment status. Each row showed invoice number, date, client name, total amount, and status badges. Tapping a row opened the full invoice detail with line items, GST breakdowns, and payment history.

The register was not just a list — it was the primary interface for invoice lifecycle management. Edit an invoice (if not yet filed in GST returns), mark as paid, delete with confirmation. The register also served as the launching point for GST exports.

## GST Export

Two export formats were implemented, matching the government-mandated structures:

Sales Register CSV — a flat export of all invoices in a date range, with columns for invoice number, date, client name, GSTIN, taxable value, CGST, SGST, IGST, and total. This format feeds directly into the accountant's workflow.

GSTR1 CSV — the format required for monthly GST return filing. B2B section with place of supply, reverse charge flag, invoice type, and rate-wise tax breakdowns. This is the format that gets uploaded to the GST portal.

Both exports used Indian FY-aware date filtering (April to March) and handled the CGST/SGST versus IGST split automatically based on client state codes.

## The Billing Spine

Phase 2 also introduced the conceptual separation that would govern all future development: the billing spine (Incoming Material → Invoice) versus the logistics spine (Gate Challan → Dispatch). These are parallel tracks, not sequential. One incoming material record can spawn multiple partial gate challans; one invoice can reference materials from multiple receiving dates. The data model was designed to support this from the start, even though the Gate Challan module was deferred to a later phase.

chapters.name: GitHub Deploy & PWA
chapters.summary: GitHub deploy + PWA + design principles doc (436 lines, 4-round QA). Items Master spec drafted.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built
Moved SEP Invoicing from local-only to GitHub Pages at rishabh1804.github.io/sep-invoicing/. Single-file architecture — sep-invoicing.html as source, index.html as deploy copy. cp + push workflow matching SproutLab and Codex conventions.

## Data Protection
Business data (21 clients, 52 invoices, 83 incoming materials, ~349KB) stays in localStorage only. .gitignore blocks all JSON files with explicit !manifest.json exception. Schism documented: GitHub JSON sync rejected in favor of manual backup/restore.

## PWA Setup
Added manifest.json (standalone display, portrait, SEP accent theme), sw.js (network-first for HTML — always get latest; cache-first for assets), generated 192/512 placeholder icons. App installable on Android.

## What Broke
1. Termux dubious-ownership fatal — git safe.directory config fix
2. manifest.json blocked by .gitignore — added !manifest.json exception

## State of the World After
SEP Invoicing has a home on GitHub. Same deploy workflow as SproutLab and Codex. Placeholder icons need replacement. docs/ directory empty — design principles doc pending.

chapters.name: Phase 7 — Stats + History Rework
chapters.summary: Stats tab rewrite: period chips, 7-card dashboard grid, inline SVG charts (bar + trend line), flippable card client drill-down with actionable insights. History tab: filter fix + date range + day headers + tappable navigation + load-more. 6,047 lines total.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

The Stats tab went from six static HTML cards to an interactive analytics dashboard. Period filtering via chip buttons (MTD/QTD/YTD/All) with Indian FY-aware YTD calculation. Seven cards in a responsive 2-column grid: Revenue Overview, Invoice States, Revenue by Client (inline SVG horizontal bar chart with tappable bars), Pending Revenue, Monthly Revenue Trend (SVG line chart with area fill), Dispatch Cycle averages, and Top Items by Volume (ranked table).

The signature feature: a flippable card overlay for client drill-down. Tapping any client bar opens a bottom-sheet overlay with a CSS 3D flip animation. Front face shows a 2x2 KPI grid (revenue, invoice count, share %, unbilled amount), state badges, and billing mode. Back face shows recent invoices, pending challans, and three action buttons — Create Invoice (pre-selects client in form), View in Register (filters to client), View in IM (filters and scrolls to challan). The flip uses a two-stage animation (rotateY 0→90° then -90°→0°) rather than true CSS 3D backface-visibility, avoiding overflow clipping issues in the overlay.

History tab got four improvements alongside the filter bug fix: from/to date range inputs, day-level group headers, tappable entries that navigate to the referenced invoice in Register or challan in IM, and a load-more button showing 50 events per batch.

## Key Decisions

Inline SVG chosen over Chart.js (zero dependencies, full CSS variable integration for dark mode) and CSS bars (insufficient control for labels and interactivity). Two-stage flip animation over true CSS 3D (overlay's overflow-y:auto and max-height clip backface-visibility content). Period filter uses createdAt timestamps, not invoice dates — consistent with 'when did we book this' rather than 'when was this dated'. Monthly trend always shows all-time data regardless of period filter — the trend needs multi-month context. Pending Revenue card is never period-filtered — unbilled material is always current state.

## What Broke

Six bugs caught during spec verification before build. The critical one: IM filtering uses _imFilter.clientId (module-level in im.js), not regFilter.imClientFilter which doesn't exist. The Jump to IM action would have silently failed. Also caught: clientId integer/string type mismatch between S.clients[].id (number) and btn.dataset.clientId (string), requiring parseInt() at the openClientDrillOverlay entry point.

## State of the World After

6,047 lines across 21 modules. Stats tab is now the operational intelligence center — period-aware metrics, visual charts, and one-tap client drill-down with actionable follow-through. History tab is a navigable activity log with filtering and cross-tab linking. The flippable card pattern is reusable for future drill-down UIs.

chapters.name: Phase 8 — Desktop Mode
chapters.summary: Desktop mode compatibility. 8A–8E complete. 8E: Clients/Items master-detail. ~7,522 lines total.
chapters.content:
## Phase 8A — Desktop Foundation

## Breakpoints & Detection

_isDesktop at 1024px, _isTablet at 768px. ResizeObserver with 150ms debounce. _pendingModeSwitch deferral prevents mode changes while overlays or challan forms are active — drains on closeOverlay() and cancelAddChallan().

## Sidebar Navigation

Icon sidebar: 64px collapsed, 240px hover-expanded with touch fallback. Body offset margin-left 64px. Bottom tabs hidden on desktop.

## Overlay Split

Centered modal with scale-in animation on desktop, bottom sheet on mobile. All existing mobile CSS untouched.

## Phase 8B — Master-Detail Container

## Split Panel Infrastructure

inv-master-detail flex container injected into #regList and #imList by renderRegisterTable() and renderIMTable(). Resizable drag handle: 280px master / 320px detail minimums, 4px wide, col-resize cursor.

## Persistence & Helpers

Per-tab width persistence in regFilter.desktopPanelWidths (ratio-based). _renderDetailEmpty() shows document SVG empty state. _restorePanelWidth() and _initDragHandle() with double-bind guard via dataset.dragInit.

## View Dispatchers

_renderRegView() and _renderIMView() replaced 19 call sites across 6 files. Global _onDragMove/_onDragEnd handlers registered once in init.js.

The 8B design contract: re-renders only update master content, detail panel untouched. This contract was refined in 8C when detail panel sync became necessary.

## Phase 8C — Register Desktop Table + Detail Panel

345 new lines across 4 files.

## Table Structure

_buildRegisterTableHtml() renders a 7-column table: checkbox (40px, always visible — _regSelectMode gate bypassed on desktop), display number (60px, monospace), client (flex), date (100px, monospace), taxable (110px, monospace, right-aligned), total (110px, monospace, right-aligned), state badge (90px). Summary bar above, export buttons below.

## Sortable Headers

data-action invDesktopSort with getRegSortConfig() as unified accessor. Desktop reads regFilter.desktopSort (lazy-initialized on first click), mobile falls back to regSortDir. Sort arrow (Unicode ▲▼) on active column only. Same column toggles direction, different column resets to desc.

## Sort Isolation

getFilteredInvoices() branches on _isDesktop. Desktop: multi-column sort (client alphabetical, date string, taxable/total numeric, state lifecycle order). Mobile: original createdAt timestamp sort preserved. Zero behavioral regression.

## Row Interaction

Cell-level data-action, not row-level. Checkbox td gets invRegToggleInv with e.stopPropagation. All other tds get invSelectRegRow → _renderRegDetail(invId). Checkbox selection and detail viewing are independent gestures.

## Detail Panel

_renderRegDetail(invId, skipMasterRefresh) renders inline in #regDetail. Content matches openInvoiceDetail() — header, client, state timeline with advance button, line items, GST totals, optional fields, action buttons. No overlay wrapper: no scrim, no pushFocus(), no body.style.overflow. Existing confirm dialogs render as centered modals on desktop.

## skipMasterRefresh Pattern

Prevents double-render. renderRegisterTable() calls _renderRegDetail with true (master just rendered). invSelectRegRow calls without skip — both master (row highlight) and detail update.

## Bug Fixes

Bug 1: _renderRegSelBar checked !_regSelectMode — always true on desktop where toggle button is CSS-hidden. Fix: (!_isDesktop && !_regSelectMode).

Bug 2: advanceInvoiceState set _tabDirty but user already on Register — no re-render. Fix: _renderRegView() after closeOverlay(). Also fixes pre-existing mobile stale-list bug.

Bug 3: 8B contract (detail untouched) meant detail went stale after edits/cancellations/deletions/filter changes. Fix: renderRegisterTable() validates _regActiveInvId — deleted → clear, filtered out → clear, visible → refresh with skipMasterRefresh=true.

## Phase 8D — IM Desktop Table + Detail Panel

Followed the 8C pattern exactly. _buildIMTableHtml() with 7 sortable columns, _renderIMDetail() for challan detail, _imActiveChallanId tracking, getIMSortConfig() for isolated desktop sort. Detail validation block in renderIMTable(). renderIMSelBar() call at end of renderIMTable().

## Phase 8E — Clients/Items Desktop Master-Detail

~426 new lines across 4 files. The hardest sub-phase — two sub-views sharing one detail panel, three render functions that write to element IDs, and element-level search bindings that get destroyed on innerHTML replacement.

## Sub-View Architecture

Clients/Items is the only tab with sub-views. The toggle sits ABOVE the master-detail wrapper on desktop (inside #clientsDesktopToggle), not inside the master panel. On mobile, the toggle is inside the sub-view HTML (unchanged). _buildClientsSubViewHtml(includeToggle) and _buildItemsSubViewHtml(includeToggle) accept a parameter — desktop passes false.

## First-Render Pattern

_renderClientsDesktop(subView) follows the 8C/8D first-render pattern: check for #clientsMasterDetail, build wrapper if missing, update master.innerHTML on re-render. Master panel uses inv-master-clients class for 35% width (narrower than Register/IM at 40%). Sub-view switch detected via master.dataset.subView — clears both _clientsActiveId and _itemsActiveId and resets detail to empty state.

## Client Detail Panel

_renderClientDetail(clientId, skipMasterRefresh) renders read-only detail in #clientsDetail. Content: header with name and active/inactive badge, GSTIN (monospace), state + code, address, billing mode, GST type, notes, rate history (sorted descending with current rate highlighted via inv-detail-rate-current), item rate overrides, recent invoices (top 5 by date with clickable rows). Action buttons: Edit (opens existing overlay) and View in Register (jumps to Register tab).

## Item Detail Panel

_renderItemDetail(itemId, skipMasterRefresh) shares #clientsDetail. Content: part number header (monospace, large), description, HSN, unit, default rate, standard weight, usage stats from _buildUsageCache() (total refs, 30-day refs, invoice lines vs challan lines). Action buttons: Edit and Delete (both open existing overlays).

## Search Binding Survival

Client search uses a global document input handler (survives re-renders). Items search uses element-level _bindItemsSearch() — destroyed on innerHTML replacement. Fix: _bindItemsSearch() called after every desktop items master render in _renderClientsDesktop().

## Bug Fixes (12 total)

Bug 1 (CRITICAL): saveClientEdit() left desktop detail panel showing stale data. Fix: refresh via _renderClientDetail() when _clientsActiveId matches.

Bug 2 (CRITICAL): addClientRate() called closeOverlay() then openClientEdit() — reopened overlay on desktop. Fix: desktop branch refreshes detail panel instead.

Bug 3 (CRITICAL): renderClientList() used invEditClient on desktop. Fix: _isDesktop conditional switches to invSelectClientRow.

Bug 4 (HIGH): saveItem()/deleteItem() left desktop detail stale/not cleared. Fix: desktop branches with _renderItemDetail() refresh and _itemsActiveId clear.

Bug 5 (HIGH): _bindItemsSearch() destroyed on master re-render. Fix: re-bind after every desktop items render.

Bug 6 (HIGH): Client search filtering didn’t validate detail panel. Fix: detail validation at end of renderClientList().

Bugs 7–12: FAB visibility, element ID preservation, sub-view toggle extraction, active state highlighting for both client and item cards.

Author docs/specs/SEP_INVOICING_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md v0.1 per REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md v1.0 §9 obligation. Solara as SEP Invoicing Builder owns. Time-bound: 30 days from Republic v1.0 ratification (2026-04-19), i.e., by 2026-05-19. Commitment chronicled on interaction-2026-04-19-001.

volumes.name: Codex
volumes.description: Library-themed PWA tracking projects, sessions, design decisions, and progress across Claude conversations. GitHub JSON storage. Shared design system roots with SproutLab but distinct icon set and library-themed tokens.
volumes.current_phase: Post-Phase 5. BN cleanup done. All content backfilled.

chapters.name: Spec & Design Session
chapters.summary: CODEX_SPEC_v1.md and CODEX_QUICK_REFERENCE.md written. Design session resolved all 3 blockers: Playfair Display + Work Sans fonts, Style C icons (19 cx() SVGs), text size system (LOW/MED/HIGH). Initial data backfill generated.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

The founding document. CODEX_SPEC_v1.md defined the complete system — data shapes for volumes, chapters, canons, rejected alternatives, TODOs, and journal sessions. The WAL (Write-Ahead Log) architecture was specified for offline-first persistence with GitHub sync. Four build phases were scoped. The Aurelius snippet format was designed as the bridge between Claude sessions and the Codex store.

CODEX_QUICK_REFERENCE.md distilled the spec into a builder’s cheat sheet: data shapes, ID formats, localStorage keys, routes, z-index hierarchy, CSS tokens, store mutation API, and build phases.

## Key Decisions

GitHub JSON storage over any backend. WAL-based sync over direct API writes. Library metaphor throughout — volumes, chapters, canons, shelves. Split architecture from day one (8 modules). The cx- CSS prefix to avoid collision with SproutLab’s zi- namespace.

## State of the World After

A complete blueprint existed. Every data shape, every route, every interaction pattern was specified before a single line of code was written.

chapters.name: Design Session
chapters.summary: Pre-build design session resolving fonts (Playfair Display + Work Sans), icons (Style C thin + accent fill, 19 icons), text size system (3-preset --fs-base driven), and initial data backfill (3 JSONs).
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Three pre-build blockers were resolved in a single session. Font pairing: Playfair Display for headings, Work Sans for body, loaded via Google Fonts CDN with fallback stacks. Icon set: Style C (thin stroke 1.5px with accent fills), 19 icons in the cx() function, brass-embossed library aesthetic. Text size system: LOW/MED/HIGH presets driven by a --fs-base CSS variable, icons fixed at 24px, persisted via localStorage.

The initial data backfill was generated — three JSON files seeding 5 volumes, 23 canons (including SproutLab’s HR-1 through HR-12), and 7 rejected alternatives.

## Key Decisions

Playfair Display + Work Sans over other pairings. Style C icons (thin + accent fill) over Style A (pure stroke) and Style B (solid fill). Three-preset text size over a continuous slider. The --fs-base approach means all font sizes scale proportionally.

## What Broke

A spec delta was identified: the QUICK_REFERENCE document had static --fs-* tokens that conflicted with the dynamic --fs-base system. Noted as a TODO, carried forward.

## State of the World After

All visual decisions were locked. The aesthetic identity — warm parchment tones, brass accents, serif headings, thin-stroke icons — was established. Ready for Phase 1 build.

chapters.name: Phase 1 — Core Build
chapters.summary: Built and deployed Phase 1. 8-module split architecture (template, styles, data, seed, core, views, forms, start). Dashboard, Volume Detail, TODOs, Settings views. Overlay/confirm/toast systems. Theme toggle, text size slider. Seed data with 5 volumes, 23 canons. PWA manifest + icons. Deployed to GitHub Pages.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

The first working Codex. Dashboard with volume cards sorted by last activity. Volume Detail with chapters, TODOs, shelf history, and danger zone. Settings with theme toggle and text size slider. Overlay, confirm dialog, and toast systems. Hash routing with breadcrumb navigation. Tab bar with icon labels. FAB with context-aware actions. All form renderers (text, textarea, select, date). 8-module split architecture with build.sh concat.

Deployed to rishabh1804.github.io/Codex/ with PWA manifest and generated book icons. Seeded with 5 volumes, 23 canons, 7 schisms.

## Key Decisions

The first build attempt diverged from spec — wrong element IDs, wrong action names, missing features. Rather than patching, the entire build was scrapped and rebuilt from the spec files. This established a principle: always build from the spec, never from memory.

Split architecture from day one rather than monolith-then-refactor. Natural page scroll over flex column layout (later reversed in Phase 2). mv over cp for the downloads workflow in Termux.

## What Broke

3 bugs: SVGs rendering at container size (fixed with explicit width/height), slider blue flash on tap (tap-highlight suppression), Chrome localhost scroll broken by flex column (removed flex, used natural scroll).

## State of the World After

4,827 lines across 10 files. A working, installable PWA with local-only storage. Ready for GitHub sync.

chapters.name: Phase 2 — Persistence Layer
chapters.summary: GitHub sync (fetchFile/pushFile/fetchAll/validateToken/flushQueue), WAL with replay, 4-step onboarding wizard, SW v2 (network-first), Settings GitHub connect/disconnect/sync, swipe navigation (canon pattern), Chrome-style pull-to-refresh. 8 hotfix rounds. ~2,400 lines across 8 modules.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

The persistence layer. GitHub sync pipeline: fetchFile, pushFile, fetchAll, validateToken, flushQueue. WAL (Write-Ahead Log) with replay on startup — every store mutation creates a WAL entry, pending entries sync to GitHub when online. 4-step onboarding wizard with GitHub connect or skip-local flow. Service worker v2 (network-first strategy). Settings upgraded with live GitHub connect/disconnect/sync controls.

Swipe navigation (adapted from SproutLab’s canon pattern) and Chrome-style pull-to-refresh with a visual spinner indicator. build.sh updated to include seed.js in the concat.

## Key Decisions

_initializing and _isRendering flags moved to data.js so all modules could access them. :where() CSS selector for zero-specificity SVG fallback sizing. Network-first service worker over stale-while-revalidate. Body flex column layout (reversing Phase 1’s natural scroll decision). Gear icon for settings over lock. No breadcrumb on settings view. Pull indicator inserted before viewContainer so it survives re-renders.

## What Broke

8 bugs across multiple hotfix rounds: SVG sizing inconsistencies (global :where() fallback), flex scroll layout issues, breadcrumb hidden attribute not applied, slider tap-highlight persisting, tab badge positioning, gear icon missing from the cx() set, breadcrumb incorrectly showing on settings, dashboard sort regression.

## State of the World After

Codex data persisted to GitHub. Changes synced automatically. The WAL ensured no data loss even when offline. ~2,400 lines across 8 modules. Phase 3 was pure UI rendering — all plumbing was in place.

chapters.name: Phase 3 — Content Views
chapters.summary: Journal view, Canons view, Canon Detail, Schisms, Snippet Import. All content rendering against existing store data.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Phase 3 turned Codex from an administrative shell into a readable knowledge base. Four major views were added: the Journal (session timeline with date grouping, volume filters, expandable detail), the Canons list (filterable by scope, category, status, with pagination and sort controls), Canon Detail (full rationale, references, supersession chains, linked schisms), and Schisms (inline below canons).

The Aurelius Snippet Import system was the pipeline breakthrough — a textarea-based JSON import with preview validation, enabling Claude sessions to push structured data (sessions, canons, schisms, TODOs, chapter updates) directly into the store.

## Key Decisions

Rendering against existing store data rather than fetching separately. Snippet import as the primary data ingestion path. Schisms rendered inline with canons rather than as a separate tab. Truncated text with expand/collapse rather than navigating to detail views for schisms.

## What Broke

7 bugs found during QA: store method validation gaps, expand animation glitches, escHtml missing on user-data innerHTML, supersession chain guard for circular references. All fixed in a dedicated QA round.

## State of the World After

Codex became a working knowledge base. Every session, canon, schism, and TODO was now readable and navigable. The snippet import pipeline meant each Claude session could export its institutional memory into Codex with a single paste.

chapters.name: Phase 4 — Polish
chapters.summary: Search, Heatmap, Stats, Trash, Error Log, Sync Detail Panel, Canon Sort. 4-round QA audit, 8 bugs fixed. Codex v1.0 feature-complete.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Six polish features completing Codex v1.0. Search with weighted scoring (canons 4x, schisms 3x, sessions 2x, volumes 1x) and 300ms debounce. A 90-day GitHub-style activity heatmap on the dashboard. Stats bar with horizontal scroll chips. Trash view for soft-deleted canons and chapters with restore and permanent delete. Error log viewer. Sync detail panel showing WAL breakdown and connection status. Canon sort controls (newest, oldest, title, scope) were added during QA when their absence became obvious.

## Key Decisions

Multi-round QA was formalized as canon-0014-multi-round-qa: four layers (Mechanical, Structural, Logic, Cosmetic), continue until layers 1–3 are zero-defect, layer 4 allows at most 2 items as documented debt. This superseded the SproutLab-scoped canon-0009.

## What Broke

8 bugs across 4 QA rounds. The critical one: replayUpdate blocked restore operations because the _deleted guard rejected payloads setting _deleted to false. Fixed by adding an exception for restore payloads. Other notable: heatmap showing oldest dates on screen (fixed with auto-scroll to scrollWidth), search overlay staying open on navigation (fixed with closeSearch at top of handleRouteChange), canon dates showing wrong months (YYYY-MM format forced to day 01, fixed in formatAbsoluteDate).

## State of the World After

Codex v1.0 was feature-complete. 4,758 lines across 8 modules. Dashboard, Volume Detail, Journal, Canons, Canon Detail, TODOs, Settings, Search, Heatmap, Stats, Trash, Error Log, Sync Panel. All deployed and live.

chapters.name: Phase 5 — Chapter Detail + Apocrypha
chapters.summary: Chapter Detail view with prose rendering, linked entities, prev/next navigation. Apocrypha section in Canons tab. Auto-migration system for zero-interaction backfill. Single-zip delivery. Self-contained repos canon.
chapters.content:
## What Was Built

Chapters evolved from metadata stubs to readable narratives. Tapping a chapter now opens a detail view with rendered prose (## headings become section titles, paragraphs preserve line breaks), auto-derived linked canons and sessions, linked TODOs, and previous/next chapter navigation. The data model gained content and order fields, with addChapter fixed to accept historical dates for backfill accuracy.

Apocrypha — the hidden texts. A collapsed section beneath Schisms in the Canons tab, holding dramatic retellings of legendary builds and visionary ideas. Three statuses: fulfilled, foretold, forgotten. Named after the tradition of texts too visionary for the official canon but preserved because someone believed they mattered.

The auto-migration system replaced snippet-based backfill for bulk data operations. CODEX_MIGRATIONS array in seed.js, runner at Step 11.5 in the boot sequence (after WAL replay, before listener registration). Data-guarded for idempotency across multiple devices.

## Key Decisions

Eight design passes refined the approach from a textarea-based editing model to a reader-first architecture. Content arrives via migrations and snippets — the app is a reader, not a writer. No content textarea in the edit form. Single-zip delivery eliminated 40 manual taps per session. Self-contained repos (docs/ directory alongside code) eliminated file uploads.

The snippet format gained new_chapters for creating chapters via import, closing a pipeline gap where chapters could only be updated, not created. Preview validation now checks for duplicates and missing targets.

## State of the World After

Codex chapters read like chapters in a book. The Codex volume itself now has 7 chapters with full narratives, navigable in sequence. The Apocrypha hold the first legendary retelling. Every future session delivers a single zip — one download, one unzip, deploy.

chapters.name: TODO Cleanup
chapters.summary: Resolved all 5 open Codex TODOs in one session. Code fixes: renderStorageUsage prefix scan (views.js), trash apocrypha section with restore/delete handlers (views.js + start.js), canon_updates in snippet pipeline (forms.js). Docs fixes: QUICK_REFERENCE reconciled with actual codebase (font tokens, KEYS, routes, store API, snippet format, build phases). Data fix: canon-0009 superseded by canon-0014 via canon_updates snippet.
chapters.content:
## The Session

Five open TODOs had accumulated across Codex’s build phases — the kind of small debts that individually seem trivial but collectively signal a project losing track of itself. This session resolved all five in one pass.

## The Fixes

TODO 1 was a documentation drift: the QUICK_REFERENCE listed static font size tokens, but the actual codebase uses a dynamic calc-based system driven by --fs-base. The fix reconciled the docs with reality and updated the KEYS section, routes table, store API, snippet format, and build phases to match the post-Phase-5 codebase.

TODO 3 was a real bug: the Storage Usage view only counted keys in the KEYS constant, missing draft keys, seed version, and migration tracking. The fix scans all localStorage keys with the codex- prefix.

TODO 4 added deleted apocrypha to the Trash view with restore and permanent delete handlers matching the existing patterns.

TODO 5 extended the snippet pipeline with canon_updates and todo_updates — enabling data corrections via snippet import instead of manual editing.

## The Method

Three QA rounds, zero bugs. Every data-action verified as wired. The session demonstrated that Codex’s patterns are stable enough that new features slot in without surprises — a good sign for a young codebase.

chapters.name: The Thirteen Scrolls Session
chapters.summary: Two cleanup sessions: (1) TODO cleanup — resolved 5 open TODOs, added renderStorageUsage prefix scan, trash apocrypha section, canon_updates + todo_updates in snippet pipeline. (2) Post-Thirteen-Scrolls cleanup — removed 110 lines of dead migration code from seed.js, updated Codex current_phase in seed, logged canon-0033 number collision as known debt.
chapters.content:
## What Was Planned

A straightforward session: resolve five open Codex TODOs, backfill SproutLab chapter narratives, and generate a session snippet. Estimated time: 90 minutes.

## What Actually Happened

The TODO cleanup went smoothly — five fixes across views.js, forms.js, start.js, and docs. Storage usage now scans all codex-* keys. Trash view shows deleted apocrypha. canon_updates and todo_updates added to the snippet pipeline. QUICK_REFERENCE reconciled. Total time: 45 minutes. No bugs.

Then came the SproutLab backfill.

## The Snippet That Was Too Large

The first attempt was a single 26KB snippet containing all 13 chapter narratives. It failed on mobile paste — the JSON was truncated at position 20,000, producing an unterminated string error. The content strings with their escaped newlines were fragile at that size.

## The Migration That Couldn’t Be Seen

Plan B: put the narratives in a migration in seed.js. The migration used store.updateChapter(), which creates WAL entries and calls _fireChange(). But _fireChange() is suppressed during initialization (_initializing flag), and the WAL entries complicated the sync cycle. The narratives were written to memory but never persisted reliably.

Plan C: direct mutation. ch.content = u.content instead of store.updateChapter(). Bypasses the WAL entirely. Simulation confirmed it worked — all 13 chapters got content. Deployed. No change on the live site.

## The Service Worker War

The SW was caching index.html. Every code change — migration fixes, SW version bumps, registration query params — was invisible because the browser kept serving the cached old code.

Attempt 1: Bump SW cache name from v2 to v3. No effect — the browser cached sw.js itself.
Attempt 2: Add ?v=3 to SW registration URL. Chicken-and-egg — the old SW serves the old code that has the old registration URL.
Attempt 3: Unregister SW via chrome://serviceworker-internals/. Appeared to work but the old code persisted.
Attempt 4: Bump migration ID to v3 to force rerun. Still no toast.
Attempt 5: Rewrite SW entirely to never cache HTML (v7). Still invisible.
Attempt 6: Clear site data via Chrome settings. Nuclear option. Cleared everything including SproutLab data.

Six attempts. None worked. The session was now three hours old.

## The Thirteen Scrolls

Plan D: abandon all machinery. Split the narratives into small individual snippets under 5KB each and import them by hand. 13 scrolls, 13 pastes, 13 confirmations.

First batch had a formatting bug — single \n after headings instead of \n\n, so renderChapterContent() merged headings with paragraphs. Everything rendered as bold headings. Regenerated all 13 with double newlines.

The user then asked for more depth — 13 individual comprehensive snippets, each one a proper narrative. Written with the weight the SproutLab journey deserved. CareTickets alone was 4.9KB of prose about a father tracking his daughter’s first illness.

Date correction caught: October 2024 should have been October 2025 (Ziva was born September 2025). Found in ch01 and ch08. Fixed.

## The Root Cause

After all narratives were imported, the user requested apocrypha edit/delete UI and a restore-from-backup feature. Both were built. Both deployed. Neither appeared on the live site.

Finally: ls -la ~/codex/index.html ~/codex/split/index.html

Root index.html: 290,888 bytes, dated April 13.
Split index.html: 313,972 bytes, dated April 14.

The zip extracted all files into split/. The build output lived in split/codex.html and split/index.html. But GitHub Pages serves the ROOT index.html. Every code change in the session — every migration, every SW rewrite, every new feature — was correctly built and committed, but the root file was never updated. The live site was running yesterday’s code the entire time.

One cp command. That was the entire problem.

## The Cost

Planned session: 90 minutes for TODO cleanup + backfill.
Actual session: approximately 6 hours across 30+ turns.

Deliverables that worked on first attempt: TODO cleanup (5 fixes), todo_updates pipeline, canon_updates pipeline. Total: 45 minutes.

Time lost to the root index.html + SW caching cascade: approximately 5 hours.

Artifacts generated and discarded: 4 migration versions (v1 through v3 plus direct mutation), 6 SW versions (v2 through v7), 3 zip deliveries that changed nothing on the live site, 8 snippet batches (first 4 with bad formatting, replaced by 4 corrected, then replaced by 13 individual comprehensive ones).

Snippet imports performed by the user on a phone: 20+. Each one required: open file, select all, copy, navigate to Settings, open Import Snippet, paste, tap Preview, tap Import, close overlay. At approximately 30 seconds each: 10+ minutes of manual work that should have been zero.

## The Fixes

build.sh rewritten to output directly to files and auto-copy to root. Running bash build.sh now produces split/codex.html, split/index.html, AND repo root index.html. No manual cp, no forgetting.

SW v7 never caches HTML. Only static assets (icons, manifest) and fonts. Every page load fetches HTML from the network. Future deploys are push + reload.

Restore from Backup added to Settings with a file picker — no copy-paste needed for large JSON files on mobile.

Apocrypha edit/delete UI added — quill and trash icons on every myth card.

Two canons enshrined: canon-0033 (root index.html must be the build output) and canon-0034 (SWs must never cache HTML). Both added to Aurelius’s permanent memory.

## The Lesson

The elaborate mechanism fails when the simple plumbing is wrong. Check the pipes before building the cathedral. A migration system, a WAL, a service worker, a snippet pipeline, an 8-pass spec process — none of it matters if the file the server reads is from yesterday.

chapters.name: Governance Founding
chapters.summary: Established the complete AI companion governance hierarchy across all three repos. Named all personas from the Order of the Codex, created the Governor tier for SproutLab, canonized the 30K Rule, and drafted 11 configuration files for Claude Code migration.
chapters.content:
## The Founding

On 15 April 2026, a simple question — 'where do we stand on these files?' — triggered the most comprehensive governance session in Codex history. Three repos were git-cloned, 74,521 lines analyzed, and every companion assignment was made through interactive MCQ decision forks.

## The Hierarchy

The governance model draws from Roman republican offices: The Consul (cross-repo oversight), Cipher the Censor (shared QA), three Builders (Aurelius, Lyra, Solara), and two Governors (Maren, Kael) for SproutLab's 61,700-line codebase.

## The Assignments

Aurelius was already named — the Chronicler who journals. Lyra (The Weaver) won SproutLab over Maren — pattern recognition over protection. Solara (The Strategist) won SEP over Theron — the CA brain over the forge metaphor. Cipher became the shared Censor. The Consul was created as a new meta-role above the Order.

## The Governor Tier

SproutLab at 61,700 lines triggered the 30K Rule. Maren (The Guardian) governs Care: home, diet, medical. Kael (The Scout) governs Intelligence: ISL, core, data, sync. Shared modules get dual review. Governors activate during QA only.

## The Eleven Scrolls

11 configuration files drafted: 4 CLAUDE.md (global + per-repo), AGENTS.md, Memory.md, PERSONA_REGISTRY.md, and 4 SKILL.md files (spec-review, qa-audit, split-file-build, snippet-import). All encoded from actual codebase analysis, not memory.

## Canons Established

Six new canons: 30K Governor Rule, QA-only activation, shared module dual review, persona reassignment process (30-day cooldown), Care vs Intelligence domain split, one-builder-per-repo binding.

chapters.name: Phase 1 — Lore Implementation
chapters.summary: Built dissertation §3.4 Lore layer — 5 categories, top-level tab, CRUD, snippet pipeline with back-compat, 3 auto-gen lifecycle hooks, trash, search. 4 governance lore entries seeded via migration. Aura deferred to Phase 1.5. +3,172 lines across 10 files.
chapters.content:
## Scope

Implement dissertation §3.4 Lore as a first-class knowledge entity, peer to Canons and Apocrypha. Backward-looking narrative wisdom in five categories.

## What Shipped (Phase 1)

Data model is dissertation-faithful: domain[], references[], tags[], sourceType, sourceId. All 5 categories present — Edicts, Origins, Cautionary Tales, Doctrines, Chronicles — with distinct voice per category rendered in detail view.

Top-level Lore tab (5th in tab bar) with category and domain filters, category-grouped sort option, italic narrative cards with per-category color-tinted left border.

Full CRUD via overlay form. Snippet pipeline accepts both {lore:[...]} and legacy {new_lore:[{op,data}]} shapes with volumes→domain and canon_id→references back-compat.

Auto-gen lifecycle hooks (proposal-only, Architect confirms): chapter abandoned → Cautionary Tale, volume archived → Chronicle, canon superseded → Doctrine. Bottom banner prompt, auto-dismisses after 20s if ignored.

Trash, search weighting (title 3x / tags 2x / body 1x, type weight 4), WAL entity_type 'lore', canons.json sync, stats bar count, autolore CSS banner.

Migration 'phase-1-lore-governance-seed-v2' seeds the 4 pre-written governance lore entries via store.addLore (v1 used direct push which bypassed WAL and never synced — hotfix PR #2).

## What Shipped (Phase 1.5 QoL)

B1 — Reference resolver extended. renderLoreDetail's references[] now resolves against canons, volumes, chapters (with parent-volume lookup), lore, apocrypha, and schisms. Each type links to its appropriate route. Unknown IDs fall back to plain text.

B2 — Lore tab health strip. Informational row above filter bar showing total count, per-category colored-dot distribution (Edicts, Origins, Cautionary Tales, Doctrines, Chronicles), and orphan counter (lore with empty references). Self-diagnostic archive health at a glance.

B3 — Markdown export. Download button in health strip outputs codex-lore-YYYY-MM-DD.md respecting active filters. Sections per category in dissertation order, voice subtitles, blockquoted prose, metadata footers. Institutional memory leaves the app.

## What Was Deferred

Aura: the 6-tier rarity system's scoring depends on Bonds, Lineage, and referenced-by counts — none of these exist as Codex primitives today. Manual dropdown was rejected as architecture debt. Deferred to Phase 1.5 A (separate session).

Bonds as first-class entity: would unlock real Aura scoring + §8.1 crystallization detection. Phase 2 territory.

Auto-lore banner silent-queue mode: wait for real-use spam signal before building.

RPG-native lifecycle hooks (Prophecy fulfilled/defied, Rite released, Ember snuffed): need those entities to exist first.

## First Cautionary Tale & Companion Doctrine

This chapter produced 'The Two Shortcuts We Didn't Take' (lore-005, Cautionary Tale) — named the distinction between environment debt (cheap, pay now) and architecture debt (expensive, defer honestly). Its descendant Doctrine 'Seed Data Must Flow Through the Sync Pipeline' (lore-006) crystallized after the v1 migration bug revealed the pattern: any write to the in-memory store that skips the sync layer is the same bug waiting to happen. This pair is the first completed knowledge lifecycle in the Codex archive — §8.1 of the dissertation happening in miniature.

chapters.name: The Forum Pattern Overhaul
chapters.summary: Six-tab Forum Pattern application (canon-0052) end-to-end. Three Province companion logs migrated to canon-0053 v1 schema. canon-gov-012 ratified (Android PWA cache invalidation). CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES + REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES drafts authored. Specs tab scaffolded as the new 6th top-level tab. Playwright harness in place: 35/35 cases green. Five Sovereign-authorized merges to main per canon-gov-011.
chapters.content:
## Scope

Apply canon-0052 Forum Pattern (Rostra → Notice Boards → Filtered Count → Stalls) across every top-level tab. Migrate the three pre-canon-0053 companion logs to v1 schema. Ratify canon-gov-012 to lock the Android PWA cache-invalidation discipline that the session itself surfaced. Author Codex's design constitution and the Republic-wide universal extract. Add Specs as a first-class entity tab.

## What Shipped

Sixteen commits across five Sovereign-authorized merges. By tab:

- **Lore** — unchanged; the pattern's reference implementation.
- **TODOs** — overhauled. Rostra with open count, per-volume colored dots (vol.domain_color), resolved tally (week/month/lifetime), 30-day resolution rate, average resolution time, overdue + stalled chips. Three filter rows (Status / Volume / Sort). Card-level overdue/stalled chips on aged TODOs.
- **Canons** — overhauled with three sub-tabs (Canons / Schisms / Apocrypha), each with its own Rostra and derived filters. Hard-coded category enum (3 values) replaced with derivation from data (7 categories now visible, including governance + builder_discipline + philosophy + deploy that the old enum hid). Lore-style colored chrome ported across (3px left border + filled chip + Rostra dot per category). Filter row labels added (Scope: / Category: / Status: / Sort:).
- **Journal** — overhauled with four sub-tabs (All / Sessions / Decrees / Logs). New build-time pipeline: scripts/extract-companion-logs.py walks docs/companion-logs/<repo>/, parses YAML frontmatter, writes data/companion-logs.json (content-addressable, no _generated_at). Logs sub-tab is the first canon-0053 consumer — surfaces all three Province logs with per-companion invocation heatmap, drift-flag count, per-province dots, GitHub blob links.
- **Library (Dashboard)** — overhauled. Rostra with volume count + per-shelf dots + cluster line (canon-cc-016 derivation: codex/sproutlab → A, sep-* → B, command-center → Monument) + signals line (active chapters / open TODOs / sessions/mo / canons). Three filter rows (Shelf / Cluster / Sort). Heatmap kept below filters; trim/remove decision deferred per canon-0052 §10 #2.
- **Specs (NEW)** — 6th top-level tab. New entity in data/specs.json with 8 hand-seeded entries drawn from on-disk docs/specs/ + docs/handoffs/. Quill icon. Per-category colored chrome (impl-spec / design-spec / canon-draft / handoff). Coverage-gap Rostra signal flags active chapters without spec_id back-pointers — the novel signal canon-0052 specifies for this tab.

## Cross-Cutting Discipline Applied

- **Reference resolver** extracted to core.js (canon-0052 cross-cutting). resolveReference + renderReferenceLink now used by Lore, Canons, and Specs cards. Plain-text references.join() pattern is now greppably zero in split/*.js.
- **Chapter status enum** normalized to nine values (planned → spec-drafting → spec-complete → in-progress → review → complete + paused/blocked/abandoned). Dashboard active count uses the canon-0052 exclusion rule. Settings has a Data Integrity section that surfaces drift (currently empty).
- **Sub-tab pattern** generic infrastructure: setSubTab action handler with localStorage persistence (codex-subtab-{tab}). Used by Canons and Journal.
- **Filter pill labels** small-caps row labels on tabs with 3+ filter rows (HR-C-10 from the design principles draft).

## Canons Touched

- **canon-gov-012 ratified** — Android-installed PWAs hold a browser-side HTML cache beyond the SW. Names the discipline that makes deploy handoffs complete by default on Android (pull-to-refresh as the third step of the deploy chain). Born from the in-session correction when the Sovereign asked 'Where are the new status icons/colours?' after a successful merge — Pages was serving the new HTML, the PWA was on the cache.
- **canon-0052** applied across 6 tabs (still draft; precedent for ratification accumulating).
- **canon-0053** ratification precondition met: three Provinces with v1-compliant logs at canonical paths (codex/, sproutlab/, command-center/).

## New Documents

- docs/specs/CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md (778 lines, draft v0.1)
- docs/specs/REPUBLIC_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md (210 lines, draft skeleton)
- docs/handoffs/CODEX_HANDOFF_SESSION_2026-04-18.md (this session's handoff)

## Test Harness

First Codex-side Playwright harness. 35 cases across 7 spec files: reference-resolver, chapter-status-enum, todos-forum-pattern, canons-forum-pattern, journal-forum-pattern, library-forum-pattern, specs-forum-pattern. python3 -m http.server as the web server (matches Codex's static-HTML shape; zero framework dependency). All green at session close.

## Cross-Province TODO Transport

command-center volume gained todo-0018-design-committee-design-principles — surfaces in the TODOs tab under the Command Center pill, queues the design committee work the Sovereign authorized for the design-principles drafts.

## What Was Deferred

Apocrypha + Schism + Companion Log card per-category coloring. Markdown ledger exports for Canons + Specs + Journal. Trash room + soft-delete UI affordance. Specs extractor pipeline. Solara/Theron cluster=B housekeeping. Markdown-section parser for the unhelpful-flag count Logs Rostra signal. canon-cc-019 Post Box drafting (queued for next session as the primary opener).

Findings become amendments to the draft before ratification.

Port the cx-canon-card / cx-lore-card per-category color treatment (3px left border + filled chip + Rostra dot) to Apocrypha cards (per-status), Schism cards (per-volume via vol.domain_color), and Companion Log cards (per-repo via vol.domain_color). Open questions §10 #2-#4 in CODEX_DESIGN_PRINCIPLES.md.

Implement canon-0052 §Delete Affordance: hidden-by-default trash icon on cards; long-press (touch) or hover-+'…' menu (desktop) reveals destructive actions; soft-delete sets _deleted + _deleted_date; Trash room in Settings with Restore + Permanent Delete; auto-clean entities where _deleted_date > 30 days. Soft-delete already exists in the data layer for canons + chapters; the UI affordance + Trash room do not.

Implement markdown ledger exports for Canons, Specs, and Journal per canon-0052 §Export Policy. Canons: category/scope/status grouping + citation references + ratification dates. Journal: sessions + decrees + logs as autobiography chapters. Specs: bundle with coverage-gap report prepended. Filename convention: codex-<tab>-YYYY-MM-DD.md. chapters.name: Pre-War Housekeeping
chapters.summary: Before the war, the house. Three pre-dawn cleanups across sibling repos to clear launch-pad cruft.
chapters.content:
## What happened

1. **SproutLab cruft purge** (PR #2 on sproutlab): removed `split/sproutlab.html` (61,948-line duplicate of the compiled entry point, same SHA as `/index.html`) and `split/.trashed-1778272350-intelligence.js` (15,278-line OS-trashed prior version). Together these had inflated Lyra's War Time brief by 77,226 lines (~2×). Real SproutLab split-module surface: ~61,933 lines, not 139,171.

2. **SEP Dashboard v2.1 baseline verification** (PR #1 merged pre-dawn): Nyx + Sovereign restored `index.html` from the 50,279-line bloat back to the 4,531-line v2.1 build. War-time Phase 1 (Pragmatic Patch) effectively completed before Hour 0.

3. **Codex PR #19 disposition**: the formal War Time declaration PR was found to be doc-only (all content already on `main` via commits `8f843d9`, `2e271a1`, `d522295`). Closed without merge; commented with the ratifying-commits pointer.

## Why it mattered

The two compiled-artifact-in-tree cases (SproutLab + SEP Dashboard) are sibling patterns of the same bug class — see Cautionary Tale `lore-compiled-artifacts-in-tree`. Cleaning both before dawn gave all three province Builders a truthful starting line.

chapters.name: Charter + Stack Selection
chapters.summary: Four decisions ratified before a single file of code was written. Edict VIII honored mechanically.
chapters.content:
## Decisions ratified (Sovereign, 2026-04-23)

1. **Temple is permanent, not one-shot** — the War Time dashboard is its first campaign, not its only one. The Temple outlasts any war.
2. **Live fetch from Codex JSON** — Codex is territory, Temple is map. Temple subscribes via `raw.githubusercontent.com`; no local cache of source-of-truth.
3. **Stack B: SvelteKit** — chosen over Stack A (Astro + Svelte islands), Stack C (React SPA), and Stack D (Vite + Svelte without SvelteKit). See Schism entries.
4. **GitHub Pages hosting** — at `https://rishabh1804.github.io/TempleOfMars/`.

## Why Stack B specifically

Astro + Svelte islands would technically ship less JS, but it imposes two mental models on a novice Builder. SvelteKit alone is one mental model end-to-end. The tell that flipped the pick: SproutLab migration scenario. SproutLab is app-shaped; Astro's islands fight that. SvelteKit fits both Temple (content-ish) and a future SproutLab rewrite (app-shaped). Learning cost dominates performance cost at this stage.

## Charter before build

README + CHARTER.md + SCHEMA.md drafted before any Svelte component code. Edict VIII honored.

chapters.name: Metrics + Capture Mechanism
chapters.summary: Priority 1 + 2 metrics locked. Capture via extension to existing snippet-import pipeline.
chapters.content:
## Metrics

**Priority 1:** Sessions per task/phase, tokens in+out, model, estimated USD cost (tokens × rate).
**Priority 2:** Wall-clock duration, commits, LOC delta, efficiency ratio (out/in).
**Priority 3 (deferred):** QA bounces, blockers, scope drift. Captured opportunistically via `log_blocker` op.

## Capture

Codex already has a snippet-import pipeline. Extended with a new `session_log` snippet type — three ops: `record_session`, `update_task_status`, `log_blocker`. Zero new infrastructure. Edict III by construction: Temple has no write credentials.

chapters.name: v0.1 Scaffold + Deploy Pipeline
chapters.summary: 41 files, 9 commits, three hotfix PRs on the deploy pipeline. Green at 23s.
chapters.content:
## What shipped

SvelteKit 2 + adapter-static + Svelte 5 runes (TypeScript strict), Tailwind v4 via @tailwindcss/vite, @vite-pwa/sveltekit (Workbox) honoring Canon 0034, SW runtime-cache for Codex raw JSON, campaign store + Codex fetchers with null-tolerant error handling, phase/campaign aggregation with USD derivation, dashboard components (Countdown, ProvinceCard, Timeline, Checkpoint, LoreSchema, CampaignSummary), design system port (Playfair + Work Sans, theme, fs-base slider, 17-glyph Icon registry), GH Actions → Pages deploy.

## Deploy fire-drill

Four failed runs before the first green. Three hotfix PRs:

1. **#2 (casing):** path base was `/templeofmars/` but GH Pages serves `/TempleOfMars/`. Assets 404'd silently.
2. **#3 (pnpm/action-setup + vite-plugin-svelte):** `version: 9` input conflicted with `packageManager` field. v4 action rejects that combo. Also: vite-plugin-svelte@^5 needs Vite 6; downgraded to ^4.
3. **#4 (setup-node cache):** `cache: pnpm` needs a lockfile to hash. We ship without one. Dropped the cache input.

Final run green at 23s.

## Streaming pattern

Initial monolithic push_files hit stream idle timeouts. Broke into 4–6 smaller batches. See Doctrine `doctrine-stream-commits-not-monoliths`.

chapters.name: Codex Integration — campaigns.json + Session v2
chapters.summary: campaigns.json declared as Temple's source; Session schema extended with optional campaign/telemetry fields.
chapters.content:
## Landed on Codex main (#20)

- `data/campaigns.json` — War Time 2026-04-24 with 8 phases across 3 provinces, 17 tasks, leads + acceptance per phase. Authoritative source; Temple reads live.
- `docs/schema/SESSION_V2.md` — draft extension to Session schema. All new fields optional (campaign_id, phase_id, task_id, builder, province, started_at, ended_at, model, tokens_in, tokens_out, commits[], loc_delta, lore_generated[]). Existing sessions render unchanged.
- `session_log` snippet type declared with three operations.

## Edict III by construction

Temple ships no credentials. The only path for session telemetry is Codex snippet-import → journal.json → raw.githubusercontent.com → Temple's SW cache. No bypass possible.
